title: VAA fetch timeout leaves redeem pill stuck in loading state
reasoning: The VAA fetch uses a 15s timeout signal. When that timeout fires while the component is still mounted, fetch rejects with a DOMException named AbortError. The catch block treats all AbortErrors as silent cancellation and returns without resetting phase or showing an error, so the pill remains in the fetching/sending/mining branch indefinitely and the user cannot retry.
reproduction: Stub /api/bridge-redeem or fetch to reject with DOMException('','AbortError') from the AbortSignal.timeout path after clicking redeem. The component remains on the fetching label instead of returning to the redeem button or showing a timeout toast.
recommendation: Distinguish component-initiated aborts from timeout aborts. For example, only silently return when ac.signal.aborted, and otherwise reset phase to idle and show a timeout/error toast. Alternatively use a named timeout controller and handle it explicitly.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests exercise unrelated address-label, auth, breach, and breaker components. There is no test for BridgeRedeemPill timeout or AbortError handling.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a jsdom test for BridgeRedeemPill that mocks fetch to reject with an AbortError while the local AbortController is not aborted, then asserts the redeem button returns and addToast receives an error.
minimumFixScope: Update BridgeRedeemPill.handleClick catch handling and add a focused component test for timeout AbortError behavior.

history.note: VAA fetch timeout AbortError now resets the pill to idle and emits an error toast unless component abort signal fired; added focused pill test.
